text.title: ref-pmbok Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for ref-pmbok. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Always show formula AND result when reporting EVM metrics. Never emit only the computed value — auditors need the formula to verify the calculation.
rule.rationale: Audit trails require reproducibility. A bare "$92K EAC" without formula cannot be verified or challenged.

rule.statement: Clamp EVM denominators to avoid division by zero. When PV or AC is zero, emit null for the affected metrics rather than infinity or NaN.
rule.rationale: LLM arithmetic on EVM rows with zero or near-zero denominators produces nonsense results that look plausible. Code calculation with explicit guards is required.

rule.statement: Present estimation accuracy bands as heuristics with ranges, never as guarantees. An agent that quotes "+/- 10%" without "bottom-up only" misleads sponsors.
rule.rationale: Sponsors anchor on the tightest accuracy band regardless of which technique was used. The technique must be named alongside the band.

rule.statement: Always pair a risk entry with exactly one response strategy. A risk without a strategy is an observation, not a managed risk.
rule.rationale: Risk registers that lack strategies are decoration. The strategy and owner are what make a risk register actionable in execution.

rule.statement: If more than 50% of projects in a portfolio are amber, the RAG threshold is miscalibrated, not the projects. Recalibrate before acting on individual project statuses.
rule.rationale: RAG inflation (everyone is amber) destroys the signal. Amber must be rare enough that it triggers genuine attention.

rule.statement: Pin the PMBoK edition in every project charter and in every agent system prompt that generates PM content. Never let an agent assume the edition.
rule.rationale: PMBoK 6 is over-represented in training data. Without explicit pinning, agents default to the 6th edition vocabulary (knowledge areas, process groups) regardless of instruction.
